title: Aureline default-target resolution vocabulary for repo, project, board, environment, and workspace contexts
description: Boundary schema for the default_target_resolution_record frozen in docs/integrations/provider_account_settings_contract.md. The record is the per-(connected_provider, surface, target_context) row every desktop, CLI, AI, work-item, publish, browser-handoff, support-export, queue-review, and admin-reconciliation surface reads to explain WHICH default target a provider-linked action will use and WHY that selection won. Default targets MUST be resolved from a closed provenance vocabulary and MUST be explainable rather than inferred from whichever provider callback arrived most recently. The schema composes with and does NOT replace schemas/integrations/provider_mapping_state.schema.json and schemas/integrations/provider_account_state.schema.json; it freezes the per-context resolution row those schemas reference. Raw URLs, raw OAuth tokens, raw delegated tokens, raw cookies, raw provider-private profile bodies, raw billing payloads, raw seat plans, and raw provider account profile bodies never cross this boundary. Adding a new target_context_class, target_provenance_source_class, selection_outcome_class, or unresolved_reason_class is additive-minor and requires a default_target_resolution_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

default_target_resolution_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this default-target resolution vocabulary. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, export, cite from support packets, queue-review rows, admin-reconciliation rollups, and AI evidence packets. Raw URLs, raw OAuth tokens, raw delegated tokens, raw cookies, raw provider-private profile bodies, and raw billing payloads never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence safe for desktop, CLI, support-export, queue-review, admin-reconciliation, and AI evidence output.

surface_class.description: Provider-linked surface class re-exported from schemas/providers/publish_later_record.schema.json and schemas/integrations/provider_mapping_state.schema.json.

default_target_resolution_class.description: Default-target resolution class re-exported verbatim from schemas/integrations/provider_mapping_state.schema.json.

target_context_class.description: Frozen per-context axis the default-target resolution applies to. repo_context: a code-host repository or fork. project_context: a code-host project, organization-level project, or planning project. board_context: an issue or planning board. environment_context: a deploy or runtime environment (e.g., release publisher, CI, environment-scoped artifact registry). workspace_context: an Aureline workspace or its bound managed workspace. Surfaces MUST disclose this verbatim rather than collapsing all default-target rows into one Connected badge.

target_provenance_source_class.description: Frozen vocabulary for candidate sources of a default target. explicit_user_choice_source: the user explicitly picked the target on this surface. repo_metadata_source: a checked-in or repo-discoverable mapping (for example, .aureline/provider-link.toml). user_default_source: the user's local default for this provider class. workspace_setting_source: the Aureline workspace's setting. admin_policy_source: an organization or workspace admin policy. inherited_org_mapping_source: an org-level mapping inherited at the workspace level. last_used_by_actor_source: the actor's most recently used target for this surface. imported_evidence_only_source: imported snapshot only; no live binding. no_source_local_only_fallback: no source resolved; the surface falls back to local-only with no provider target. Surfaces MUST list every candidate source they considered, with a selection_outcome_class on each, instead of inferring the winning target from a single provider callback.

selection_outcome_class.description: Frozen per-candidate outcome explaining why a candidate source did or did not win. Exactly one provenance_chain entry MUST carry selected_default_target unless the resolution class is no_default_local_only_fallback (in which case every candidate carries a non-selected outcome and the record MUST cite an unresolved_reason_class).

unresolved_reason_class.description: Frozen reason vocabulary explaining why no candidate source produced a default target. no_unresolved_resolution_succeeded is the only legal value when default_target_resolution_class is anything other than no_default_local_only_fallback (i.e., a target was selected).

outcome_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence explaining the outcome verbatim (for example, Selected because admin policy forces this target; user cannot widen).

linked_source_record_ref.description: Opaque ref to the source record (repo metadata file, user-default record, workspace setting record, admin policy record, org mapping record, last-used-by-actor record, imported snapshot). Empty when target_provenance_source_class is no_source_local_only_fallback.

candidate_target_ref.description: Opaque ref to the candidate provider-side target this source proposed. Empty when the candidate could not be resolved (for example, dropped_due_to_broken_target or dropped_due_to_account_unresolved).

provenance_chain_entry.description: One candidate provenance source considered while resolving the default target. Surfaces MUST cite every candidate source they considered, with a selection_outcome_class on each, so the resolution is reviewable rather than inferred.

linked_provider_account_settings_record_.description: Opaque ref to the provider_account_settings_record from schemas/integrations/provider_account_state.schema.json this resolution belongs to. Empty only when no settings record exists yet (for example, an admin-reconciliation rollup over imported evidence).

context_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence describing the context the default target applies to (for example, Default repo for code-host actions in this workspace).

local_scope_ref.description: Opaque ref to the local Aureline scope (workspace, repo, project, board, or environment) the resolution is bound to.

default_target_explanation.description: Short reviewable sentence explaining how the winning default target was resolved. Required so provenance is auditable rather than inferred from the most recent provider callback.

selected_target_ref.description: Opaque ref to the resolved provider-side target (repo, project, board, environment, or workspace). Empty when default_target_resolution_class is no_default_local_only_fallback.

provenance_chain.description: Ordered list of every candidate provenance source considered, with the selection_outcome_class for each. Exactly one entry MUST carry selected_default_target unless default_target_resolution_class is no_default_local_only_fallback.

narrowed_by_admin_policy.description: True when admin policy narrowed or forced the resolution below what user-default or repo-metadata mappings otherwise permit.

metadata_safe_export_only.description: When true, this resolution may only be exported with metadata-safe defaults regardless of any wider redaction class. cached-shadow account states, policy_blocked, expired_seat continuity, and offline_with_cached_read_state MUST raise this to true.

linked_default_target_resolution_record_.description: Opaque ref to a previous default_target_resolution_record this record supersedes. Empty when no prior resolution existed.

linked_account_switch_audit_record_ref.description: Opaque ref to the account_switch_audit_record that minted or transitioned this resolution. Empty when no audit event was responsible (for example, the first resolution captured for a new surface).

allOf.description: default_target_resolution_class no_default_local_only_fallback MUST set unresolved_reason_class outside no_unresolved_resolution_succeeded, leave selected_target_ref empty, and forbid any selected_default_target outcome in the provenance chain.
default_target_resolution_class outside no_default_local_only_fallback MUST set unresolved_reason_class to no_unresolved_resolution_succeeded and provide a non-empty selected_target_ref.

default_target_resolution_class admin_forced MUST set narrowed_by_admin_policy true and have a provenance_chain entry whose target_provenance_source_class is admin_policy_source and selection_outcome_class is selected_default_target.

default_target_resolution_class repo_metadata_inherited MUST have a provenance_chain entry whose target_provenance_source_class is repo_metadata_source and selection_outcome_class is selected_default_target.

default_target_resolution_class user_default_inherited MUST have a provenance_chain entry whose target_provenance_source_class is user_default_source and selection_outcome_class is selected_default_target.

default_target_resolution_class workspace_setting_inherited MUST have a provenance_chain entry whose target_provenance_source_class is workspace_setting_source and selection_outcome_class is selected_default_target.

default_target_resolution_class explicit_user_choice MUST have a provenance_chain entry whose target_provenance_source_class is explicit_user_choice_source and selection_outcome_class is selected_default_target.

default_target_resolution_record.description: Per-(connected_provider, surface, target_context) row explaining which default target a provider-linked action will use and why. The provenance_chain MUST list every candidate source considered with a typed selection_outcome_class so reviewers can audit the decision. Default-target resolution stays consistent across AI, work-item, publish, and browser-handoff surfaces by reading this record rather than inferring the winning target from individual provider callbacks.
